Lead.description: Lead is a toxic metal that can leach into drinking water from plumbing materials, especially older service lines, solder, and fixtures.
Lead.whyAvoid: Even low levels can harm brain development and learning in children and can contribute to high blood pressure and kidney problems in adults.

Copper.description: Copper can enter drinking water when household plumbing corrodes, particularly in newer copper pipes or when water is acidic.
Copper.whyAvoid: Higher exposure can cause stomach upset and, over time, may contribute to liver or kidney issues in sensitive individuals.

Total Trihalomethanes (TTHMs).description: TTHMs are a group of disinfection byproducts that can form when chlorine or other disinfectants react with natural organic matter in the source water.
Total Trihalomethanes (TTHMs).whyAvoid: Long-term exposure has been associated with increased cancer risk and potential effects on the liver and kidneys.

Chloroform.description: Chloroform is one of the trihalomethanes that can form as a disinfection byproduct when drinking water is treated with chlorine.
Chloroform.whyAvoid: Long-term exposure is linked with increased cancer risk and can stress the liver and kidneys.

Bromodichloromethane.description: Bromodichloromethane is a trihalomethane disinfection byproduct formed when disinfectants react with organic material in water, especially when bromide is present.
Bromodichloromethane.whyAvoid: Long-term exposure may increase cancer risk and may impact liver and kidney health.

Dibromochloromethane.description: Dibromochloromethane is a trihalomethane formed as a disinfection byproduct during water treatment.
Dibromochloromethane.whyAvoid: Long-term exposure may increase cancer risk and may contribute to liver and kidney effects.

Bromoform.description: Bromoform is a trihalomethane that can form as a disinfection byproduct in chlorinated drinking water.
Bromoform.whyAvoid: Long-term exposure may increase cancer risk and can add to overall disinfection byproduct burden.

Total Haloacetic Acids (HAA5).description: HAA5 are a group of haloacetic acids that form as disinfection byproducts when chlorine reacts with natural organic matter.
Total Haloacetic Acids (HAA5).whyAvoid: Long-term exposure has been associated with increased cancer risk and potential reproductive and developmental effects.

Nitrate.description: Nitrate is an inorganic compound that can enter water from fertilizer runoff, animal waste, septic systems, or natural deposits.
Nitrate.whyAvoid: High levels can reduce oxygen delivery in infants ("blue baby" syndrome) and may contribute to thyroid-related concerns.

Nitrite.description: Nitrite is related to nitrate and can enter water through the same sources or form from nitrate conversion.
Nitrite.whyAvoid: Elevated nitrite can interfere with oxygen transport in the blood, which is especially risky for infants and sensitive populations.

Fluoride.description: Fluoride may be naturally present or added for dental health.
Fluoride.whyAvoid: At higher exposures, fluoride can contribute to dental fluorosis and, in extreme cases, skeletal effects.

Arsenic.description: Arsenic is a naturally occurring element that can dissolve into groundwater from certain rock formations.
Arsenic.whyAvoid: Long-term exposure is associated with increased cancer risk and can affect the skin, cardiovascular system, and other organs.

Turbidity.description: Turbidity measures how cloudy water is due to tiny particles. It is often used as an indicator of filtration and treatment effectiveness.
Turbidity.whyAvoid: High turbidity can signal problems with treatment and can make it easier for microbes to persist in water.

Total Coliform Bacteria.description: Total coliform are a group of bacteria used as an indicator to assess water system integrity and potential pathways for contamination.
Total Coliform Bacteria.whyAvoid: Their presence can indicate a higher risk that disease-causing organisms could enter the water system, even if total coliform themselves are not usually harmful.
